The DB incorrectly merges 'Jensen, Chief Justice, concurring.' into [¶13] and places the III section header after [¶12] instead of before it.  The DB text shows the merged form occurs exactly once; the corrected form matches the PDF source precisely.
note: This is a structural ingestion defect affecting paragraph-level organization. The justice names in the PDF are on separate indented lines; the correction preserves them on a single line (consistent with rest of DB format) while restoring the proper boundary between [¶13] and the concurrence header.

new_exact:
[¶10] No stipulation or agreement was filed with the district court. Further, this case is different from Brakke in that Tim Hamburger did not provide any excusable reason for the delay in moving to modify primary residential responsibility, which did not occur until May 2021 (20 months after T.L.H. began residing with him). Thus, we conclude the court did not err by refusing to retroactively terminate Tim Hamburger's child support obligation or recognize an obligation by Kimberly Hamburger.

old_exact: Thus, we conclude the court did not err in denying Tim Hamburger child support from May to September 2021. IV[¶14] Tim Hamburger states the district court erred by denying him an evidentiary hearing. He does not support his statement with any law or argument. We conclude he waived this issue on appeal. See Riemers v. Grand Forks Herald, 2004 ND 192, ¶ 11, 688 N.W.2d 167 ("[A] party waives an issue by not providing supporting argument and, without supportive reasoning or citations to relevant authorities, an argument is without merit.").
new_exact:
Thus, we conclude the court did not err in denying Tim Hamburger child support from May to September 2021.

IV

[¶14] Tim Hamburger states the district court erred by denying him an evidentiary hearing. He does not support his statement with any law or argument. We conclude he waived this issue on appeal. See Riemers v. Grand Forks Herald, 2004 ND 192, ¶ 11, 688 N.W.2d 167 ("[A] party waives an issue by not providing supporting argument and, without supportive reasoning or citations to relevant authorities, an argument is without merit.").
note: Section IV heading fused with ¶14. PDF shows ¶13 ending with '2021.' on line 219, then blank line 220, then 'IV' alone on line 221, then blank line 222, then '[¶14]' on line 223. DB incorrectly fuses 'IV' and '[¶14]'.

source: 2022ND147.pdf via pdftotext, paragraph 12 (Marble v. State five-part test listing)
evidence: The DB text contains a severely garbled version of the five-part test from Marble v. State, 2015 MT 242, where the numbered list items are shuffled and jumbled out of order. The PDF source shows a properly formatted, legible five-part numbered list with correct ordering: (1) evidence discovered since trial, (2) failure to discover sooner not due to lack of diligence, (3) material to issues at trial, (4) neither cumulative nor merely impeaching, (5) indicates reasonable probability of different outcome. The DB version has items out of order and intermingled text fragments that are incoherent.
